data.title: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation

RQa.note: The paper is a preprint affiliated with Apple, linked to a code repository on GitHub.
RQa.evidence: Apple. https://github.com/apple/ml-ssd

RQb.note: The solution, SSD, is a general-purpose method for improving code generation in LLMs.
RQb.evidence: Can a large language model (LLM) improve at code generation using only its own raw outputs... We answer in the affirmative with simple self-distillation (SSD)... it generalizes across Qwen, Llama, and GPT-OSS models at 4B–30B scale... it yields substantial LiveCodeBench v6 gains across six models

RQc.note: The code repository is available on GitHub, but the text does not specify the license, cost, or usage terms, so it is unspecified.
RQc.evidence: https://github.com/apple/ml-ssd

RQd.note: The method has some technical limitations, such as requiring specific hyperparameter tuning to balance precision and exploration, and its understanding relies on a local ideal-fit approximation for analysis.
RQd.evidence: SSD improves Qwen3-30B-Instruct from 42.4% to 55.3% pass@1 on LiveCodeBench v6... gains are not model-specific... SSD does not collapse diversity... The best observed setting... uses Ttrain=2.0, Teval=1.1, and training-time top-k=10... the optimal Teff generally shifts towards a higher temperature... Evaluation-time behavior under a local ideal-fit approximation

RQe.note: The solution is presented as a mature method with evidence of substantial and consistent improvements across multiple models and benchmarks, suggesting it is production-ready.

More broadly, these results suggest that strong code models contain latent capability that can be unlocked without a verifier, a teacher, or reinforcement learning.
